metadata.summary: A lightweight propgrid in wxpython to show/config data

documentation: **propgrid** is a lightweight wxPython class to show/config data 1. Install ```bash $pip install propgrid ``` 2. Usage ```python ... g = pg.PropGrid(self) # Separator g.Insert(PropSeparator().Label('general')) # Text g.Insert(PropText().Label('string').Value('hello world!').Indent(1)) ``` See the **demo.py** for more details.

classes.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator.Clone
documentation: Return a new validator for the same field of the same object.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator.SetObject
documentation:
Set or change the object with which the validator interacts.

Useful for changing objects when a single panel is used
to edit a number of identical objects.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator.TransferToWindow
documentation:
Transfer data from validator to window.

Delegates actual writing to destination widget to subclass
via _setControlValue method.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator.TransferFromWindow
documentation:
Transfer data from window to validator.

Delegates actual reading from destination widget to subclass
via _getControlValue method.

Only copies data if value is actually changed from attribute value.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator.Validate
documentation:
Validate the contents of the given control.

Default behavior: Anything goes.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator._setControlValue
documentation:
Set the value of the target control.

Subclass must implement.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.BaseValidator._getControlValue
documentation:
Return the value from the target control.

Subclass must implement.

Base class for validators that validate *attributes* of
objects and provide two-way data transfer between those
attributes and UI controls.

BaseValidator has no knowledge of the specific
type of widget to be validated. Responsibility for
interacting with specific types of widgets is delegated
to subclasses.

Subclasses must implement the following methods:
    * _setControlValue(self, value)
            Set the value of control to the value provided.
    * _getControlValue(self)
            Return the value of control.
Remaining logic is implemented in BaseValidator.


CHAMELEON POWERS:

To simplify use in an object-editor context (in which
a single editor GUI is used to edit multiple objects
of the same type), BaseValidator allows changing
objects on the fly.

For example, if references to field widgets are stored
in a list self._fieldWidgets, the editor class could
implement the following methods:

    def SetObject(self, editObject):
        "Set object for editing."
        self._editObject = editObject
        self._updateValidators()
        self.TransferDataToWindow()

    def _updateValidators(self):
        for wgt in self._fieldWidgets:
            validator = wgt.GetValidator()
            validator.SetObject(self._editObject)


FORMATTERS

BaseValidator uses Formatters for validation and
two-way reformatting. A Formatter is aware of source data type,
and handles translation between presentation representation and
storage representation.

A Formatter must provide the following interface methods:
    * format(stored_value)
            Format a value for presentation.
    * coerce(presentation_value)
            Convert a presentation-formatted value
            to a storage-formatted value (may include
            type conversion such as string->integer).
    * validate(presentation_value)
            Validate a presentation-formatted value.
            Return True if valid or False if invalid.

If a formatter is assigned, the BaseValidator
will call the formatter's format, coerce, and validate
methods at appropriate points in its processing.

With a formatter, there is no need of, for example,
integer-aware entry fields. The formatter handles
validation and conversion of the input value.

classes.name: propgrid.validators.SelectorValidator

documentation:
Set the value *and the options* of the control.
By the time this is called, the value is already mapped for display.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.SelectorValidator._getControlValue
documentation: Return the value from the TextCtrl.

Validator for ControlWithItems widgets (ListBox, Choice).

For wx.ListBox, assumes single-selection mode (wx.LB_SINGLE).

methods.name: propgrid.validators.CheckListBoxValidator._setControlValue

documentation:
Return the value from the TextCtrl.

Returns a list of client data values (not row indices).

methods.name: propgrid.validators.CheckListBoxValidator._setControlOptions
documentation:
Set up or update control options.

@param options:    Sequence of (id,label) pairs.

methods.name: propgrid.validators.CheckListBoxValidator._setControlSelections
documentation:
Select the specified items in the control, and unselect others.

@param value:    Integer or sequence of integers representing
                the data value(s) of item(s) to be selected.

                If None or empty sequence, all currently-selected
                items will be deselected.

                Any items in value that are not found in the
                control have no effect.

modules.name: propgrid.enumtype
documentation: This enumerated-values class is intended for use in module-level constants,
such as an employee type or a status value. It allows simple attribute
notation (e.g., employee.Type.Serf or employee.Type.CEO), but also supports
simple bidirectional mapping using dictionary notation (e.g.,
The software is licensed according to the terms of the PSF (Python Software

classes.name: propgrid.enumtype.EnumType
documentation:
Enumerated-values class.

Allows reference to enumerated values by name
or by index (i.e., bidirectional mapping).

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.FormatterMeta
documentation:
Metaclass for subclasses of Formatter.

Each instance class MUST define either validate(self, value) method
or re_validation regular expression string.

If the latter, the validate method will be autogenerated.

If re_validation is defined, validate method is overridden to
validate the string value against the regular expression.

If re_validation_flags is defined, the flags will be
used when the re_validation regular expression string is compiled.

Each instance class MAY define:
-    format(self, value) method.
-    coerce(self, value) method.

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.ObjectIdFormatter
documentation: Object ID is assumed to be a large (32 bit?) unsigned integer.

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.MoneyFormatter
documentation: Assumes decimal money, but doesn't assume currency.

methods.name: propgrid.formatters.PathFormatter.is_pathname_valid
documentation:
`True` if the passed pathname is a valid pathname for the current OS;
`False` otherwise.

methods.name: propgrid.formatters.PathFormatter.is_path_creatable
documentation:
`True` if the current user has sufficient permissions to create the passed
pathname; `False` otherwise.

methods.name: propgrid.formatters.PathFormatter.is_path_exists_or_creatable
documentation:
`True` if the passed pathname is a valid pathname for the current OS _and_
either currently exists or is hypothetically creatable; `False` otherwise.

This function is guaranteed to _never_ raise exceptions.

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.DateFormatter
documentation:
Date string (YYYY-MM-DD).

Storage format:      wx.DateTime
Presentation format: YYYY-MM-DD

Accepts only YYYY-MM-DD format (allows variant separators '/' and '.').
Accepts dates in range (1000-2999)-(01-12)-(01-31).
Leading zeros optional in month and day.
Does not enforce # of days in month.

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.DateFormatterMDY
documentation:
Alternate date string (MM-DD-YYYY).

Presentation format: MM-DD-YYYY

Accepts only MM-DD-YYYY format  (allows variant separators '/' and '.').
Accepts dates in range (01-12)-(01-31)-(1000-2999).
Leading zeros optional in month and day.
Does not enforce # of days in month.

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.TimeFormatter
documentation:
Time string (12-hour or 24-hour format, with or without seconds or am/pm).

Storage format:      HH:MM:SS -- 24-hour format.
Presentation format: HH:MM    -- 24-hour format.

Accepts 12-hour or 24-hour format, with or without seconds or am/pm.

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.TimeFormatter12H
documentation:
Alternate time string (12-hour format, without seconds).

Storage format:      HH:MM(:SS) -- 24-hour format.
Presentation format: HH:MM(aa) -- 12-hour format.
                     (aa may be 'am' or 'pm')

classes.name: propgrid.formatters.DateTimeFormatter

methods.name: propgrid.formatters.DateTimeFormatter.__init__
documentation: Presentation format: same as storage format.

Date/time string.

Uses a DateFormatter and a TimeFormatter.
